threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'Last stop!' and everyone suddenly vanishes at a random junction.
Last night on the 196, I saw a guy quietly playing an entire saxophone solo. No one batted an eyelid, just the usual 2am vibe. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a dad on the playground teaching his kid to swear in Turkish while the mom was on her phone, blending cultures in real-time.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar that serves cocktails in vintage flowerpots. It’s the perfect blend of quirky and cozy, just like our Kiez.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they roll their eyes at tourists taking pics outside La Taqueria while ignoring the line outside El Farolito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 late last night, and a guy serenaded us with a ukulele. For a moment, the whole bus felt like a scene from a movie – very SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just hop on any tram and see everything; they miss the charm of getting lost in the narrow streets where locals actually live.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their confused faces at the price of a café amb llet – still thinking it should be under 1 euro like at home.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'single' means a cup of coffee, not an all-day breakfast. Bonus points if you ask for iced coffee in February.
Caught the night bus from Peckham last week; saw a guy arguing with a seagull over a chip. Only in South London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I watched a kid ride their bike past the kebab shop, weaving through the market stalls while the scent of spices filled the air.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you grab a currywurst at 3 AM, served by a guy in a bathrobe looking like he just rolled out of bed. Real late-night charm.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can tell you the exact time a Muni delay will go from bad to worse, like clockwork.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, saw a Muni bus stop for a pigeon crossing the street—traffic jammed up while the bird took its sweet time. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's only charm is its plazas, but the real treasure is the tiny bar by the corner that’s been pouring the same vermut since the 70s.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they hesitate at the crosswalk—still waiting for the 'green man' to appear instead of just following the locals.
